generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic speed controller for motor actuation, converting desired speed parameters into low-level motor voltage commands for the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)."
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo actuator controlled via Actuation Functional Entity, translating steering angle commands into physical steering movements for the OME."
]

instance ActuationFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Converts high-level operational parameters (e.g., speed, steering angle) into low-level actuation commands for physical actuators like the Vedder Electronic Speed Controller and Steering Servo."
]

instance DataCollectionAndDeviceControlEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Handles bidirectional data exchange and remote control of physical actuators, enabling services like Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) with real-time adjustments and latency constraints."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution LiDAR sensor collecting 3D point cloud data for object detection and environmental mapping, with a maximum network latency of 100 ms."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth-sensing camera capturing spatial data for object detection and vehicle state estimation, synchronized with LiDAR data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance MotorStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor monitoring motor parameters (e.g., RPM, torque) for real-time vehicle state tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStateData
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Precision sensor measuring steering wheel angle and vehicle yaw rate for navigation and control systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleData
]

instance VehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Speed sensor providing real-time vehicle velocity data for adaptive cruise control and collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleSpeedData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data processed into 2D plane coordinates via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking software component."
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
    base:desc "Depth-sensing data synchronized with LiDAR for enhanced object detection and environmental mapping."
]

instance MotorStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStateSensor
    base:desc "Real-time motor operational parameters (RPM, torque) transmitted for vehicle state monitoring."
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
    base:desc "Steering angle and yaw rate data for navigation and control systems."
]

instance VehicleSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedSensor
    base:desc "Vehicle velocity data for adaptive cruise control and collision avoidance systems."
]

instance ObjectTracksAndDrivingPath : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
    base:desc "Processed object tracks and driving path data derived from raw LiDAR and depth camera inputs for real-time visualization."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component converting raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks for collision avoidance and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational model integrating motor, steering, and speed data to provide a unified vehicle state representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured point cloud data after preprocessing (noise filtering, clustering) for object detection and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network
]

instance DepthCameraDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed depth-sensing data aligned with LiDAR data for enhanced environmental mapping."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network
]

instance MotorStateDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Normalized motor state data (RPM, torque) for real-time vehicle diagnostics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateModel, ROS2Network
]

instance SteeringAngleDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calibrated steering angle and yaw rate data for navigation systems."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateModel, ROS2Network
]

instance VehicleSpeedDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered vehicle speed data for adaptive cruise control and collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleSpeedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateModel, ROS2Network
]

instance ObjectTracksAndDrivingPathProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and driving path data for real-time 3D visualization and decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTracksAndDrivingPath
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Network : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 network enabling modular and interoperable communication between software components in the Digital Twin system."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionAndDeviceControlService, ActuationControlService, RealTimeVisualizationService
]

instance DataTranslationComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions for consistency across heterogeneous data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionAndDeviceControlService, ActuationControlService
]

instance ThreeDVisualizationComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Hardware/software component rendering real-time 3D visualizations of vehicle state, object tracks, and driving paths."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationService
]

instance WiFiUDPCommunication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi communication protocol using UDP for low-latency data transmission between Physical Twin Entity and Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionAndDeviceControlService, ActuationControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataCollectionAndDeviceControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating raw sensor data and transmitting actuation commands to physical components with a maximum 100 ms latency."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, ActuationCommandAction
]

instance ActuationControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing real-time adjustments to physical actuators (e.g., speed controller, steering servo) based on digital twin commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationFeedbackInsight, EmergencyBrakingAction
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing immersive 3D visualizations of vehicle state, object tracks, and driving environment for operators."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalAwarenessInsight, CollisionRiskInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time digital representation of vehicle state (speed, steering, motor parameters) synchronized with physical twin."
]

instance ActuationFeedbackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Feedback on actuator performance (e.g., motor response time, steering precision) for system calibration."
]

instance EnvironmentalAwarenessInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Spatial awareness of surrounding objects and driving path derived from LiDAR and depth camera data."
]

instance CollisionRiskInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of potential collision risks based on object tracks and vehicle trajectory."
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated command generation for physical actuators (e.g., speed adjustments, steering corrections)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Safety-critical action triggering emergency braking to prevent collisions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance CyberBiophysicalDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital twin environment integrating cyber-physical components with biological/environmental elements for real-time monitoring and control."

]

]

]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DTS involves control commands generated by the **Digital Twin Entity** and executed on the **Physical Twin Entity** via the **Actuation FE** and **Controlling FE**. Key components and flows include:
  - **Command Types**:
    - **Motor Actuation**: Commands to adjust motor speed or torque, derived from adaptive cruise control (ACC) logic.
    - **Steering Servo Actuation**: Commands to adjust steering angles, integrated with path-planning algorithms.
    - **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control)**: Dynamic speed adjustments based on projected driving paths and object tracks.
  - **Control Message Formats**: Commands are serialized and translated by the **Data Translation** component (ISO 23247 compliant) to ensure compatibility between software entities. The exact message format is not specified but follows ROS2 message standards for intra-entity communication.
  - **Feedback Systems**: The **Actuation FE** provides real-time feedback (e.g., motor/servo state) to the Digital Twin for closed-loop control. This feedback is integrated into the **Controlling FE** for adaptive adjustments.
  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Not explicitly described in the document. The system relies on the **Data Translation** component for data integrity but lacks details on cryptographic validation or authentication protocols.
  - **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly described. The system likely prioritizes safety-critical commands (e.g., emergency braking) over non-critical adjustments, but no explicit arbitration mechanism is mentioned.
  - **Command Execution Confirmation**: Assumed to occur via feedback loops in the **Actuation FE**, though confirmation protocols (e.g., acknowledgment messages) are not detailed in the document.
  - **Communication Path**: Control signals are transmitted from the Digital Twin to the Physical Twin via the **Wi-Fi network** (as inferred from inter-entity communication), with intra-entity routing handled by ROS2."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by standards but not implemented due to hardware constraints. The system does not explicitly specify varying time scales for different services or latency requirements beyond this synchronization threshold, but the **ROS2-based implementation** implies real-time constraints for sensor-actuator loops and user-facing components (e.g., 3D visualization and control panel)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates coordination via an **ISO 23247 RA-compliant architecture**, where software components (Functional Entities, FEs) are mapped to **ROS2 packages** and deployed across distributed hardware (e.g., FEs for 3D visualization, control panels, or sensor-actuator loops). The **Physical Twin Entity** acts as a central hub for resource management (compute, sensors, actuators), integrating general automotive functionalities like *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*. Component integration relies on **ROS2 nodes** within packages, enabling inter-entity communication (e.g., data exchange between Digital Twin Entity and Physical Twin Entity). The architecture deviates from ISO 23247 by **hosting general functionalities in the Physical Twin Entity**, potentially impacting ownership and engineering processes. No explicit system-wide coordination protocol (e.g., consensus algorithms) is mentioned beyond ROS2’s publish-subscribe model."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** infrastructure is defined as follows:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-Entity (ROS2 Network)**: All software components within the Digital Twin Entity communicate using **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)**, a middleware framework designed for real-time data exchange and modular software development.
   - **Inter-Entity (Wi-Fi Network)**: Communication between the **Physical Twin Entity** (vehicle) and the **Digital Twin Entity** occurs over a **Wi-Fi network**. The transport layer protocol is partially specified as **UDP** (inferred from the truncated text 'using U' in the document), which is suitable for low-latency, high-throughput data transmission.

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - Not explicitly described in the document. The system implies a **distributed architecture** with clear separation between the **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**, but specific topology (e.g., star, mesh) is not detailed.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - The system enforces a **maximum network latency of 100 ms** from the vehicle to the Digital Twin Entity, ensuring real-time responsiveness for critical applications like adaptive cruise control (ACC) and emergency braking.
   - Bandwidth requirements are not explicitly quantified but are implied to be high due to the transmission of **LiDAR point clouds**, **vehicle state data**, and **actuation commands**.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - Not explicitly described in the document. The reliance on **ROS2** and **Wi-Fi** suggests some inherent redundancy (e.g., ROS2's topic-based communication), but no explicit failover or backup protocols are mentioned.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - Not explicitly described in the document. No encryption, authentication, or access control mechanisms are detailed for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** for this DTS is primarily **edge-based**, with computation offloaded to the Digital Twin to minimize latency:
  - **Hosting Platforms**:
    - **Edge Deployment**: The Digital Twin runs on an **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** (or similar embedded platform), enabling real-time processing of sensor data and control commands. This aligns with the document’s emphasis on offloading computation to the DT for low-latency operation.
    - **On-Premise**: The Physical Twin Entity (sensors/actuators) operates on the vehicle’s onboard systems, with no mention of cloud integration.
  - **Computational Requirements**:
    - **Point Cloud Processing**: The **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking** component demands significant computational resources for real-time LiDAR data processing.
    - **Control Logic**: The **Controlling FE** (e.g., ACC algorithms) requires deterministic execution, likely implemented in C++ or ROS2-native libraries.
    - **Visualization**: The **3D Visualization** and **Presentation FE** rely on GPU acceleration (implied by the use of embedded platforms like Jetson Orin, which supports CUDA), though this is not explicitly stated.
  - **Storage Requirements**:
    - **SQL Database**: Used for structured storage of processed data (e.g., reports, historical sensor logs), as indicated in Table I.
    - **Volatile Memory**: Real-time data (e.g., LiDAR scans, vehicle state) is likely stored in RAM for low-latency access.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Not explicitly described. The system appears tailored for a single vehicle, with no mention of horizontal scaling (e.g., multiple DT instances) or dynamic resource allocation.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**:
    - **Single-Instance Deployment**: Optimized for one vehicle’s Digital Twin, with no indication of multi-vehicle or fleet-wide deployment.
    - **Hybrid Edge-Cloud**: Not supported; the document focuses solely on edge deployment with no cloud integration.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
    - **Redundancy**: Not explicitly described. The document does not mention backup systems, redundant sensors, or failover mechanisms for the Digital Twin.
    - **Deterministic Execution**: ROS2’s real-time extensions (e.g., DDS with QoS policies) may ensure predictable latency, but specifics are not provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard for Digital Twin frameworks in manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines:
  - **Entity-based reference architecture** with standardized roles for **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**.
  - **Interoperability standards**: While not explicitly named, the standard implies compatibility with **industry protocols** like **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange between entities and physical systems.
  - **Compliance frameworks**: The standard itself serves as a **certification reference**, ensuring alignment with **manufacturing digital twin best practices**. No additional certifications (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62443) are mentioned.
  - **Information exchange guidelines**: Part 4 of ISO 23247 (not detailed here) likely covers **data formats, APIs, and semantic interoperability**, but the provided context focuses on **Part 2’s architectural compliance**.
  - **Domain specificity**: The standard is tailored to **manufacturing**, with entities designed for **equipment, facilities, products, personnel, and processes**, ensuring **vertical industry alignment** rather than generic DT frameworks.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) must adhere to **ISO 15622:2018** performance requirements, which define safety-critical operational constraints for ACC functionality. The system's design must ensure **real-time data collection** of the ego vehicle’s state (e.g., speed, acceleration, braking) and **external data** (e.g., traffic conditions, road infrastructure) to maintain situational awareness. While the documents emphasize the **safety-critical nature** of the system, they do not specify detailed cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, intrusion detection) or fail-safe mechanisms beyond the **100 ms maximum network latency requirement** (per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 Release 15**). The system’s compliance with **functional safety standards (e.g., ISO 26262)** is implied but not explicitly detailed in the provided references. No explicit mention of **access control, authentication, threat mitigation, or secure communication channels** (e.g., DSRC, AES-256) is found."
]
